title: CEO Commentary on Loan Condition – As of December 31, 2026
content:
Total loans stood at $6,657,948,000 as of March 31, 2026, reflecting a modest increase of 0.2% from the previous quarter and remaining stable year-over-year with no change over the prior fiscal period.

Portfolio Composition:
The primary concentration within the loan portfolio is anchored by New Vehicle loans at $1,577,118,000 (23.7%) and Used Vehicle loans at $1,425,649,000 (21.4%). Together, these two segments represent 45.1% of the total portfolio.

The remaining loan categories include First Lien Real Estate ($1,878,223,000 or 28.2%), Junior Lien Real Estate ($539,506,000 or 8.1%), Commercial Loans/Lines of Credit Secured by Owner Occupied Nonfarm Property ($524,766,000 or 7.9%), All Other Unsecured loans including Payday Alternative and Unsecured Credit Card segments totaling $36,167,000 (combined), and other smaller categories such as Leases Receivable which had a balance of zero.

Delinquency & Credit Quality:
The credit union reported total delinquent loans at $498,524,000. The largest exposure within the delinquency portfolio is concentrated in Used Vehicle loans ($316,775,000), followed by New Vehicle loans ($145,124,000).

Delinquency concentration diverges from overall portfolio composition; while both segments are significant parts of the total loan book, delinquent amounts for these two categories combined account for 63.7% of all delinquent balances. Management continues to focus on credit quality through underwriting and monitoring.
